title: spergula nodosa var. glandulosa
intro: Spergula nodosa var. glandulosa, also known as the glandular spurrey, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is primarily found in the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
